title: humboltia expansa
intro: Humboldtia expansa, also known as the Indian Poison Nut or Poison Nut, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant contains a toxic compound called calabishine, which is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Humboldtia expansa has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal benefits.
